# BEMServer Core

{{ pypi }} {{ python }} {{ build-status }} {{ pre-commit }} {{ coverage }} {{ doc }}

BEMServer is a free Building Energy Management software platform.

Its purpose it to store data collected in buildings and produce useful information such as performance indicators or alerts.

modules.name: bemserver_core.process.degree_days
documentation: Heating / Cooling Degree Days computations

functions.name: bemserver_core.process.degree_days.compute_dd
documentation:
Compute heating/cooling degree days

:param Series air_temp: Outside air temperature
:param string period: One of "day", "month", "year"
:param string type_: Type of degree days to compute ("heating" or "cooling")
:param int|float base: Base temperature

:returns Series: Heating degree days

Note: base unit must match air_temp unit

functions.name: bemserver_core.process.degree_days.compute_dd_for_site
documentation:
Compute heating/cooling degree days for a given site

:param datetime start_dt: Time interval lower bound (tz-aware)
:param datetime end_dt: Time interval exclusive upper bound (tz-aware)
:param string period: One of "day", "month", "year"
:param string type_: Type of degree days to compute ("heating" or "cooling")
:param int|float base: Base temperature
:param string unit: Unit to express the result

:returns Series: Heating degree days

Note: base unit must match unit

functions.name: bemserver_core.migrations.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: bemserver_core.migrations.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

Check that the "val_in" type matches the expected property type.

functions.name: bemserver_core.database._generate_ddl_trigger_read_only
documentation:
Generate the SQL statement that creates an "update read-only trigger"
on a specific column for a table.

:param str table_name: The name of the table concerned by the trigger.
:param str col_name: The name of the column to protect from update.
:returns sqlalchemy.DDL: An instance of DDL statement that creates the trigger.

documentation:
Make table columns read-only

:param list fields: List of model Columns

classes.name: bemserver_core.database.Base

methods.name: bemserver_core.database.Base._apply_sort_query_filter
documentation:
Add order_by instruction to query.

sort_field is a field name, optionally prefixed with "+" or "-".
    No prefix is equivalent to "+", which means "ascending" order.

methods.name: bemserver_core.database.Base._add_sort_query_filter
documentation:
Add sort argument to query function

sort must be a list of fields to sort upon, by order of priority
    (the first field is the first sort key). Each field is a field
    name, optionally prefixed with "+" or "-". No prefix is equivalent
    to "+", which means "ascending" order.

methods.name: bemserver_core.database.Base._add_min_max_query_filters
documentation: Add min/max query filters feature to query function

methods.name: bemserver_core.database.Base._add_in_field_search_query_filters
documentation:
Add "in field search" feature to query function

This is a "SQL like" filter, used to search if a string is contained in any of
the column (field) values, insensitive case.

methods.name: bemserver_core.database.Base._filter_bool_none_as_false
documentation:
Filter query by boolean value where Null is considered False

```
param sqlalchemy.orm.Query query

Query to filter

param sqlalchemy.schema.Column

Field to filter on

param boolean value

Whether to get True or False/Null values
```

Returns filtered query.

classes.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.get_last
documentation:
Get timeseries last values

:param datetime start_dt: Time interval lower bound (tz-aware)
:param datetime end_dt: Time interval exclusive upper bound (tz-aware)
:param list timeseries: List of timeseries
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
:param str timezone: IANA timezone to use for first/last timestamp
:param str inclusive: Whether to set each bound as closed or open.
    Must be "both", "neither", "left" or "right". Default: "left".
:param string col_label: Timeseries attribute to use for column header.
    Should be "id" or "name". Default: "id".

Returns a dataframe.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.get_timeseries_stats
documentation:
Get timeseries stats

:param list timeseries: List of timeseries
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
:param str timezone: IANA timezone to use for first/last timestamp
:param string col_label: Timeseries attribute to use for column header.
    Should be "id" or "name". Default: "id".

Returns a dataframe.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO._convert_from
documentation:
Convert data to given units

:param DataFrame data_df: DataFrame to convert
:param list ts_l: List of Timeseries objects
:param str col_label: DataFrame column labels: IDs or names
:param dict convert_from: Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit

Converts column for each item in convert_from dict.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO._convert_to
documentation:
Convert data to given units

:param DataFrame data_df: DataFrame to convert
:param list ts_l: List of Timeseries objects
:param str col_label: DataFrame column labels: IDs or names
:param dict convert_to: Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit
:param string src_unit: Unit to use as source unit for all timeseries

Converts column for each item in convert_to dict.

If src_unit is provided, it is used as source unit for all timeseries
in place of their respective units. This is useful for aggregated data
where the result of the aggregation may not have the same unit as the
original data (e.g. count).

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.set_timeseries_data
documentation:
Insert timeseries data

:param DataFrame data_df: Input timeseries data
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
:param Campaign campaign: Campaign
:param dict convert_from: Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit to convert
    timeseries data from

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO._fill_missing_and_reorder_columns
documentation:
Add missing columns and reorder colums

- Add missing columns (columns with no values in DB)
- Ensure columns are in the order of the timeseries list parameter.
    The SQL query may return them in any order.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.get_timeseries_data
documentation:
Export timeseries data

:param datetime start_dt: Time interval lower bound (tz-aware)
:param datetime end_dt: Time interval exclusive upper bound (tz-aware)
:param list timeseries: List of timeseries
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
:param dict convert_to: Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit to convert
    timeseries data to
:param str timezone: IANA timezone
:param str inclusive: Whether to set each bound as closed or open.
    Must be "both", "neither", "left" or "right". Default: "left".
:param string col_label: Timeseries attribute to use for column header.
    Should be "id" or "name". Default: "id".

Returns a dataframe.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.get_timeseries_buckets_data
documentation:
Bucket timeseries data and export

```
param datetime start_dt

Time interval lower bound (tz-aware)

param datetime end_dt

Time interval exclusive upper bound (tz-aware)

param list timeseries

List of timeseries

param TimeseriesDataState data_state

Timeseries data state

param int bucket_witdh_value

Value of the bucket width.
Must be at least 1.

param str bucket_witdh_unit

Unit of the bucket width
One of "second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", "month", "year".

param dict convert_to

Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit to convert
timeseries data to

param str aggregation

Aggregation function.
One of "avg", "sum", "min", "max" and "count".

param str timezone

IANA timezone

param string col_label

Timeseries attribute to use for column header.
Should be "id" or "name". Default: "id".
```

The time alignment of the buckets respects the timezone.

Note: `start_dt` and `end_dt` may have timezones that don't match
`timezone` parameter. The conversion is done internally. In practice,
though, it might not be the most intuitive way to use this function.

Returns a dataframe.

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataIO.delete
documentation:
Delete timeseries data

:param datetime start_dt: Time interval lower bound (tz-aware)
:param datetime end_dt: Time interval exclusive upper bound (tz-aware)
:param list timeseries: List of timeseries IDs or names
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
Base class for TimeseriesData IO classes

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataCSVIO.import_csv
documentation:
Import CSV file

:param srt csv_data: CSV as string
:param TimeseriesDataState data_state: Timeseries data state
:param Campaign campaign: Campaign
:param dict convert_from: Mapping of timeseries ID/name -> unit to convert
    timeseries data from

If campaign is None, the CSV header is expected to contain timeseries IDs.
Otherwise, timeseries names are expected.

documentation:

See `TimeseriesDataIO.get_timeseries_data`.

documentation:

methods.name: bemserver_core.input_output.timeseries_data_io.TimeseriesDataJSONIO.import_json

If campaign is None, the JSON header is expected to contain timeseries IDs.
Otherwise, timeseries names are expected.

modules.name: bemserver_core.model.notifications
documentation: Notification

functions.name: bemserver_core.model.notifications.notification_after_insert
documentation:
Callback executed on insert event

Email notification. Since the email is sent in a separate thread, we want to
ensure the notification is committed to database, so we register a callback
to run on the next commit event of the session.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25078815/

documentation: Send notification email

functions.name: bemserver_core.model.notifications.init_db_events_triggers

This function is meant to be used for tests or dev setups after create_all.
Production setups should rely on migration scripts.

methods.name: bemserver_core.model.notifications.Notification.get_count_by_campaign

    Defaults to None, which means count all notifications.

methods.name: bemserver_core.model.notifications.Notification.mark_all_as_read
documentation:
Mark notifications as read

:param int user_id: User for which to mark notifications as read.
:param int campaign_id: Only mark notification as read for this campaign.
    Defaults to None, which means all campaigns.

modules.name: bemserver_core.time_utils
documentation: Time utils

functions.name: bemserver_core.time_utils.floor
documentation:
Floor datetime to a given time period

:param str period: Period in
    ["second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", "month", "year"]
:param int period_multiplier: Period multiplier.

Period multipliers are only allowed for fixed size periods (second, minute, hour).
If using multipliers, note that the origin of the period is on Jan 1st 1970.
This can lead to surprising results if the multiplied period is not a divider of
above period. E.g. "4 hours" starts at midnight, while "5 hours" may not.

functions.name: bemserver_core.time_utils.ceil
documentation:
Ceil datetime to a given time period

:param str period: Period in
    ["second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", "month", "year"]
:param int period_multiplier: Period multiplier.

Period multipliers are only allowed for fixed size periods (second, minute, hour).
If using multipliers, note that the origin of the period is on Jan 1st 1970.
This can lead to surprising results if the multiplied period is not a divider of
above period. E.g. "4 hours" starts at midnight, while "5 hours" may not.

functions.name: bemserver_core.time_utils.make_date_range_around_datetime
documentation:
Make date range around datetime

:param datetime datetime: Timezone aware datetime
:param str period: Period in
    ["second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", "month", "year"]
:param int period_multiplier: Period multiplier.
:param int periods_before: Number of periods before datetime
:param int periods_after: Number of periods after datetime

Returns a tuple of timezone aware datetimes.
